metadata.title: Why a Stationary State Is Secretly Spinning
metadata.note: Clean rebuild. Global phase rotates at angular frequency E/hbar while |psi|^2 stays fixed. No erroneous cycles-per-second conversion.

source_of_truth: beat_sheet.json — edit the sheet, not this file

beats.label: This is Liam, in for Bear. A stationary quantum state is not frozen. Its wave function rotates conti

beats.prompt: Start with what stationary means operationally. Repeat the same position measurement on identically prepared systems at different times. The probability histogr
beats.label: Start with what stationary means operationally. Repeat the same position measurement on identically

beats.prompt: If the measurable distribution stands still, what exactly is spinning?
beats.label: If the measurable distribution stands still, what exactly is spinning?

beats.prompt: An energy eigenstate separates into a spatial shape phi of x and a time factor e to the minus i E t over h bar.
beats.label: An energy eigenstate separates into a spatial shape phi of x and a time factor e to the minus i E t

beats.prompt: At every point, that time factor is a unit-length arrow in the complex plane. Its angle advances at angular frequency E over h bar, while its length remains one
beats.label: At every point, that time factor is a unit-length arrow in the complex plane. Its angle advances at

beats.prompt: Multiplying the whole state by that phase rotates every complex amplitude through the same angle. It changes the clock hand, not the spatial shape.
beats.label: Multiplying the whole state by that phase rotates every complex amplitude through the same angle. It

beats.prompt: Probability uses psi star times psi. The rotating factor and its complex conjugate cancel, leaving the fixed magnitude squared of phi of x.
beats.label: Probability uses psi star times psi. The rotating factor and its complex conjugate cancel, leaving t

beats.prompt: Picture a clock hand above the floor. The hand rotates, but its length casts the same shadow. The wave function is the hand; the probability density is the fixe
beats.label: Picture a clock hand above the floor. The hand rotates, but its length casts the same shadow. The wa

beats.prompt: A single global phase cannot be observed by itself. Every expectation value of a time-independent observable is unchanged for one energy eigenstate.
beats.label: A single global phase cannot be observed by itself. Every expectation value of a time-independent ob

beats.prompt: But combine two different energies and their phase arrows rotate at different rates. Their relative angle changes, so interference terms can make the probabilit
beats.label: But combine two different energies and their phase arrows rotate at different rates. Their relative

beats.prompt: That is the boundary of the word stationary. One energy eigenstate has a fixed probability density. A superposition of different energies generally does not.
beats.label: That is the boundary of the word stationary. One energy eigenstate has a fixed probability density.

beats.prompt: The state is a spinning clock whose shadow holds still. Stationary describes the observable distribution, not an unmoving complex wave function.

Stationary is the shadow. The quantum clock never stops.
